channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Trump and Iran Face Off in Iran War Negotiations
item.description: As the United States and Iran make a second attempt at a deal, their negotiating styles are on a collision course.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: The Touska, the Iranian-Flagged Ship Seized by U.S. Forces, Was Under Sanctions
item.description: Marines are searching thousands of containers aboard the Touska, an Iranian cargo ship that the Navy disabled and seized on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Strait of Hormuz off the coast of Musandam, Oman, last week.

item.title: Israeli Soldier in Lebanon Sledgehammered a Statue of Jesus
item.description: The military is investigating the soldier. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu expressed regret for any hurt caused to “believers in Lebanon and around the world.”
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger

item.title: Tim Cook Will Step Down as Apple C.E.O.
item.description: The longtime leader of the iPhone maker will be replaced by John Ternus, the company’s head of hardware engineering.
item.dc:creator: Kalley Huang and Tripp Mickle
item.media:description: Tim Cook joined Apple in 1998 and was its chief operating officer under the company’s co-founder Steve Jobs.

item.title: Who Is John Ternus, Apple’s Low-Profile Leader?
item.description: John Ternus, a low-profile but influential executive at Apple, could be next in line to replace the company’s longtime chief executive, Tim Cook, if he steps aside.
item.dc:creator: Kalley Huang and Tripp Mickle
item.media:credit: Photo Illustration by Klawe Rzeczy; Source Photographs by George Etheredge for The New York Times; Juan Arredondo for The New York Times; Amy Osborne for The New York Times; Lucia Vazquez for The New York Times; Jim Wilson/The New York Times; Shutterstock

item.title: Lori Chavez-DeRemer, Trump’s Labor Secretary, Steps Down Amid Internal Investigation
item.description: Lori Chavez-DeRemer was facing multiple scandals and an internal inquiry into charges of misconduct.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Davis O’Brien
item.media:credit: Al Drago for The New York Times
item.media:description: Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer has been accused of having an affair with a member of her security team and using department resources for personal trips.

item.title: Kash Patel Sues The Atlantic for $250 Million Over Article Claiming Excessive Drinking
item.description: Mr. Patel accused the publication of defamation, asking for $250 million in damages. A spokeswoman for The Atlantic called the suit “meritless.”
item.dc:creator: Katie Robertson
item.media:description: Kash Patel, the F.B.I. director, sued The Atlantic.

item.title: Democrat Betty Yee Leaves the California Governor’s Race
item.description: Xavier Becerra, who once struggled to gain traction, has found growing support after Eric Swalwell left the race amid sexual harassment allegations.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall
item.media:credit: Mike Kai Chen for The New York Times
item.media:description: Xavier Becerra, the former California attorney general, has had a resurgence after the sudden departure of Eric Swalwell from the governor’s race.

item.title: Why a Democratic Senate, Once Unthinkable, Is a Real Possibility
item.description: Helped by a favorable national environment and strong candidate recruitment, Democrats are tied or ahead in four Republican-held seats, polls show.
item.dc:creator: Nate Cohn
item.media:credit: Ash Adams for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mary Peltola could help overcome a brand problem for Democrats in Alaska.

item.title: Tracking the Virginia Redistricting Referendum Early Vote
item.description: Voters in Virginia will decide on Tuesday whether to redraw the state’s congressional map in an attempt to eliminate all but one safe Republican district.
item.dc:creator: Jonah Smith and Christine Zhang

item.title: How Bruce the Parrot Landed Atop the Pecking Order, Without a Beak
item.description: The kea gained fame for learning to use a pebble to groom himself. Scientists were astounded by his next innovation.
item.dc:creator: Carl Zimmer

item.title: Canadian Tourist Is Killed in Shooting at Mexico’s Pyramids
item.description: A gunman opened fire at the Teotihuacán archaeological site outside Mexico City before killing himself, the authorities said. Six Americans were among those injured, including two from gunshots.
item.dc:creator: Emiliano Rodríguez Mega and Cyntia Barrera Díaz
item.media:description: Police officers at the Pyramid of the Moon at the Teotihuacán pyramids in Mexico after a deadly shooting on Monday.

item.title: Louisiana Killer’s Threats to Himself and Others Hinted at Future Violence
item.description: Shamar Elkins had been suffering severe mental health problems, his family said. His deadly rampage, which killed eight children, has profoundly scarred a community facing rising domestic violence.
item.dc:creator: Eduardo Medina, Christina Morales and Jesus Jiménez
item.media:description: People attend a candlelight vigil in Shreveport, La., on Sunday.

item.title: Singer D4vd Charged With Murder of Celeste Rivas Hernandez
item.description: The singer is accused of sexually abusing Celeste Rivas Hernandez before killing the teenager with a sharp weapon and mutilating her body. He has pleaded not guilty.
item.dc:creator: Matt Stevens and Emmanuel Morgan
item.media:description: The singer D4vd, 21, created the anthem for Fortnite. The severely decomposed remains of Celeste Rivas Hernandez were discovered in his car’s trunk in September.

item.title: The Onion Signs New Deal to Take Over Infowars
item.description: A new deal, which would allow The Onion to use the Infowars name and website address, must be approved by a Texas judge.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Mullin and Elizabeth Williamson
item.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Onion, a satirical news outlet, wants to convert the right-wing Infowars site into a parody of itself.

item.title: London Braces for Disruption From Tube Drivers’ Strike
item.description: The dispute over working conditions and hours is expected to cause widespread delays, stopping service on two lines.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.media:description: A sign warning travelers of strike-related delays at the Stratford Underground station in London on Monday.

item.title: Japan to Sell More Weapons Abroad, Breaking With Postwar Pacifism
item.description: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi reversed limits on arms exports as Japan faces rising threats from China and unpredictability from its main ally, the United States.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Chang W. Lee/The New York Times
item.media:description: A Japanese marine unit takes part in exercises in Tokunoshima, Japan, in 2023.

item.title: ‘Earthset’ Is Captured on Video for First Time
item.description: The Earth slips behind the moon in an iPhone video taken by Reid Wiseman during NASA’s Artemis II mission, a sequel to the iconic Earthrise photo taken by Apollo 8.
item.dc:creator: Adeel Hassan

item.title: Blue Origin’s Failure May Hamstring NASA’s Moon Plans
item.description: The space agency is counting on Jeff Bezos’ company to deliver equipment essential to the next moon landing, only two years away.
item.dc:creator: Kenneth Chang
item.media:description: Blue Origin’s New Glenn rocket lifting off from Cape Canaveral, Fla., on Sunday. While the launch was successful, the deployment of its payload, a communications satellite, was not.

item.title: A Day in the Life of New York City’s Young Chess Stars
item.description: Children from New York schools are consistently winning national chess championships, and programs dedicated to teaching young people the game are booming.
item.dc:creator: Emily Belz and Sara Naomi Lewkowicz
item.media:description: Ayden Spellman, 11, is one of the top young chess players in New York City.

item.title: The Trump Administration Is Coming After Birth Control Access in a Terrifying New Way
item.description: The new Title X guidance from the Trump administration mentions contraception only once.
item.dc:creator: Jill Filipovic

item.title: Can Kevin Warsh Pass the Jerome Powell Test?
item.description: The question of the central bank’s independence looms over the economy.
item.dc:creator: David Wessel

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel Refers to Kash Patel’s Job in the Past Tense
item.description: The late night host called him “our soon-to-be former F.B.I. director” after publication of an article in The Atlantic alleging excessive drinking and unexplained absences.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)

item.title: Second Jury Finds Uber Responsible for Sexual Assault by a Driver
item.description: The ride-hailing giant has now lost the first two of more than 3,000 pending federal lawsuits.
item.dc:creator: Emily Steel
item.media:credit: Joseph Seif for The New York Times
item.media:description: Uber faces scrutiny across the country as lawmakers, investors and others move to hold the company accountable for sexual violence during rides.

item.title: Gilgo Beach Killer Told His Wife That He Murdered 7 Women in Their Home
item.description: Rex Heuermann, who pleaded guilty to murdering eight women, told his wife that he killed seven of the victims in a downstairs room in their Long Island home, she said in a Peacock documentary.
item.dc:creator: Andy Newman
item.media:description: Asa Ellerup, the ex-wife of the convicted Gilgo Beach serial killer Rex Heuermann, said Mr. Heuermann was “very nervous” when he talked about the murders.

item.title: George Ariyoshi, 100, America’s First Governor of Asian Descent, Dies
item.description: A Democrat, he served three terms as Hawaii’s governor (1974-86), rising out of a tough district of Honolulu. He tried to diversify an economy heavily dependent on tourism.
item.dc:creator: Robert D. McFadden
item.media:description: George R. Ariyoshi speaking in Honolulu in 1977, during his first term as Hawaii’s governor. His election upended a tradition of white and Republican control in the state.

item.title: ‘Oh, My Baby’: Mother Mourns Son, 15, Whose Killing Was Filmed on Video
item.description: Jaden Pierre was fatally shot at a Queens park in broad daylight, and bystanders captured the shooting on video. Hundreds attended a vigil for him on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Andy Newman and Nate Schweber
item.media:credit: Victor J. Blue for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mourners place candles at a memorial during a community vigil to remember Jaden Pierre at Roy Wilkins Park in Queens on Monday.
